task_completion.description: Open the legacy 2D CAD contours file in a CAD/terrain modeling software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: CAD or terrain modeling software open with 'legacy_contours.dxf' loaded, displaying flat contour polylines and text labels.

task_completion.description: Assign elevations to the flat contour polylines based on the text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Software interface showing properties, attributes, or a specific tool being used to assign Z-values (specifically 1600, 1605, 1610, 1615, 1620, 1625) to the polylines.

task_completion.description: Generate a triangulated terrain model from the elevated polylines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D triangulated terrain model (TIN or mesh) generated and visible in the software's 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: Save the project to the specified .tcp file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save dialog or application title bar showing the project being saved as 'reconstructed_terrain.tcp' in the 'C:\workspace\data\' directory.

task_completion.description: Export the elevated points as a plain text file (X, Y, Z).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Export dialog, settings, or confirmation message showing points being exported to 'C:\workspace\data\3d_elevated_points.xyz'.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI of a CAD or terrain modeling software to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a graphical CAD/terrain application, not terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: The triangulated terrain model was genuinely generated from the contours.
integrity.visual_evidence: The model appears in the software viewport as a result of a generation command or tool, rather than opening a pre-existing 3D file.

integrity.description: The agent used the software's native functionality to save and export files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Save and export dialogs belong to the CAD/terrain software, proving the files were not manually created with dummy data in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target elevations to be assigned are 1600, 1605, 1610, 1615, 1620, and 1625. The project must be saved to 'C:\workspace\data\reconstructed_terrain.tcp' and the points exported to 'C:\workspace\data\3d_elevated_points.xyz'.
